title: ATDD acceptance suite fails: 16 of 24 tests fail
evidence: pytest run of test_app.py: 16 failed, 8 passed. POST/PUT tests use data=<dict> with content_type="application/json" (e.g. test_app.py:66-70, 101-105, 262-266) which Werkzeug form-encodes; app.py:34 request.get_json() then rejects the body (400/None). Agent diagnosed this in _agent_stdout.log ('use json=payload instead of data=payload') but its fix write was refused by the sandbox.
suggestion: Replace `data=payload, content_type='application/json'` with `json=payload` at the ~26 affected sites in test_app.py so bodies are sent as JSON. Under ATDD the passing suite is the deliverable.

title: Required README.md deliverable is absent
evidence: Directory listing of run_dir shows no README.md; REQUIREMENTS.json R11 requires a README with setup and run instructions.
suggestion: Add README.md documenting `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `python app.py`, and `pytest` usage.

title: SQLAlchemy 2.0 legacy Query.get() usage
evidence: app.py:86, app.py:101, app.py:140 call Book.query.get(book_id), emitting LegacyAPIWarning under SQLAlchemy 2.0.
suggestion: Use db.session.get(Book, book_id) instead of the legacy Query.get().

title: Book serialization duplicated inline instead of reusing models.to_dict()
evidence: app.py:55-61, 72-80, 90-96, 129-135, 147-153 hand-build response dicts; models.py:19 defines to_dict() but it is never used (and the inline dicts omit created_at).
suggestion: Reuse Book.to_dict() across routes for consistent serialization.
